project_goal: Master 3D immersive asset creation for scroll-driven, Apple-style interactive websites using a video-to-frame workflow.

workflow_pipeline.step_1_generation: Create 3D immersive motion videos focusing on depth, lighting, and camera movement.
workflow_pipeline.step_2_extraction: Convert videos into sequential image frames (JPG/WebP) via tools like EZGIF or FFmpeg.
workflow_pipeline.step_3_implementation: Map scroll progress to a frame INDEX using useScroll + useTransform, then draw that frame to a <canvas> via a useMotionValueEvent('change') listener calling drawImage(). Note: useTransform alone animates DOM styles, not canvas frames, so the change-listener step is required.

generation_tools.primary: Google Flow / Veo - fast iteration, strong for color, lighting, and camera-driven scenes.
fallbacks: Seedance 2.0 - strongest temporal consistency; use when an object must stay identical frame-to-frame (assembly, exploded views).
Higgsfield - camera-control presets (orbit, dolly, crane) map directly onto the C/S-curve camera pillar.
generation_tools.blender_note: Blender is NOT a forced graduation. Only fall back to it if scrub-testing reveals morphing that no AI model fixes. With correct prompts, AI video has handled exploded sequences in practice.

technical_constraints.optimal_frame_count: 100 to 150 frames per 5-second sequence to balance smooth animation with web performance.
technical_constraints.target_file_formats: WebP or highly optimized JPG to minimize initial asset load size and prevent scroll lag.
technical_constraints.css_requirement: Use 'position: sticky' on wrapper containers to lock the product in the viewport during scroll animation.
technical_constraints.aspect_ratio: Decide the web target ratio BEFORE generating (16:9 desktop hero, 9:16 mobile, or 1:1). AI tools output fixed ratios - choosing late means regenerating.
technical_constraints.scrub_validation: Never judge a clip by playback alone. Always scrub it slowly frame-by-frame; morphing invisible at 24fps can surface under manual scroll. This is the real pass/fail test.

creative_pillars.camera_movement: Prioritize sweeping camera arcs (C or S curves) around the subject over static object spinning to maximize the illusion of 3D depth.
creative_pillars.motion_pacing: Maintain slow, deliberate, and smooth movements so the animation remains fluid when scrubbed manually by a user's scroll speed.
creative_pillars.lighting_and_contrast: Utilize studio lighting setups, rim lights, and soft dramatic shadows to establish physical presence and a premium texture feel.
creative_pillars.composition: Design for continuous flow without abrupt cuts, focusing on smooth transitions, floating elements, or modular product assemblies.

learning_practice.per_video_log: For every video, record: the prompt used, which tool, what worked, and what failed. This log is where the creative eye actually compounds - without it, 50 videos is just 50 clips.
learning_practice.goal: Build a transferable vocabulary (camera moves, depth, atmosphere, mood), not just a folder of renders.

the_50_video_roadmap.objectives: Animate simple shapes (spheres, cubes) bouncing or sliding. Master studio lighting, reflections, and realistic gravity on basic surfaces.
Introduce dynamic camera sweeps, viewpoints flying through objects, and orbital paths to practice environmental parallax.
Experiment with high-end premium materials (brushed titanium, liquid chrome, matte finishes) and shifting light colors mid-motion.
Execute complex, multi-part interactive concepts like abstract product assembly or exploded views tailored for an immersive web experience.
